goal: Land the test-runner-mcp (G8 Testing+CI, P0 priority) external MCP plugin manifest as a sibling test inside the existing `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block in tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs (next to the existing mcp_pytest_runner test from WO-0077), with a real-subprocess `tools/list` health-check integration test asserting at least one canonical multi-language test-runner tool is advertised. Reverses DEC-0021's premature deferral per DEC-0024 (the canonical multi-language test-runner MCP server is shipped as `test-runner-mcp` on npm by privsim — not under the `mcp-test-runner` / `test-runner` / `mcp-server-test-runner` names the prior planner triad searched on PyPI/GitHub).
plan_summary: Solo-feature G8 plugin-manifest landing WO mirroring WO-0080 (ruff G7 solo-feature deferral-revival) verbatim, retargeted from G7-quality namespace to G8-testing namespace and from PyPI/uvx install path to npm/npx install path. Add plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml + scripts/devtools/install-test-runner-mcp.sh + replacement scripts/verify/P3-W11-F07.sh + ONE new test `test_runner_manifest_health_check` inserted into the existing `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block in crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs (between the existing `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` at line ~199 and the module's closing `}` at line 200, additive only — DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out for the small ~80-LOC commit). Tier-1 install path: `npx -y test-runner-mcp@<PINNED-SHA-OR-VERSION>` per DEC-0024 §Decision point 2 (mirrors WO-0076 ESLint pattern of `npx -y @eslint/mcp@0.3.5` — npm-managed package self-resolves Node + transitive deps). DEC-0024 §Decision point 4 prescribes SHA-pinning DEFENSIVELY (16 GitHub stars + 13 commits = light upstream maintenance — protect against silent semver violations); executor MAY substitute a frozen semver tag if live capture confirms the latest tag exposes the canonical 6-tool surface (bats/pytest/flutter/jest/go/cargo per DEC-0024 §Decision point 3). Tier-2 fallback (per DEC-0024 §Rationale + Decision point 4 — executor authority granted): if Tier-1 live capture fails, executor MAY vendor a ~200-line TypeScript wrapper at `plugin/test-runner-mcp/` (NEW directory; granted under forbidden_paths exemption) using `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` shelling out to `cargo nextest`, `pytest`, `vitest`, `go test`, `bats`, `flutter test`, `jest`, etc. Pre-baked M1/M2 mutation contract per the 8-WO mature pattern (WO-0066/0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0077/0079/0080). F07 has `attempts=0` — first WO attempt; clean execution expected per the SEXTUPLE-verified G3→G4→G5→G6→G7→G8 paired-manifest template.
scope_in: 1. PRIMARY (Tier-1) install path per DEC-0024 §Decision point 2: `npx -y test-runner-mcp@<PINNED-REF>` — mirroring the WO-0076 ESLint shape `npx -y @eslint/mcp@<PINNED-VERSION>`. Per DEC-0024 §Decision point 4, the `<PINNED-REF>` SHOULD be a SHA-pin (e.g., `git+https://github.com/privsim/mcp-test-runner.git#<COMMIT-SHA>`) for defensive immutability; alternatively, a frozen semver tag from npm (e.g., `test-runner-mcp@0.1.5` if that tag exists and exposes the canonical 6-tool surface) is acceptable when live capture confirms the canonical bats/pytest/flutter/jest/go/cargo tool set per DEC-0024 §Decision point 3. The fallback `test-runner-mcp` (no version pin) is FORBIDDEN per AC15 + master-plan §13 + .claude/rules/commit-style.md (latest != stable canonical surface). Capture the chosen ref via `npm view test-runner-mcp version` (latest semver) AND `gh api repos/privsim/mcp-test-runner/commits --jq '.[0].sha'` (latest commit SHA) at WO-execution time; document both in the manifest top-of-file rustdoc + RFR §Tier decision section.

2. SECONDARY (Tier-2) fallback path per DEC-0024 §Rationale + §Revisit-trigger (executor authority granted to escalate to Tier-2 in the SAME WO if Tier-1 smoke fails — avoids an extra WO cycle): vendor a ~200-line TypeScript wrapper in NEW directory `plugin/test-runner-mcp/` (granted under forbidden_paths exemption — UCIL has zero vendored TS MCP wrappers prior to this WO; this would be net-new infrastructure with the precedent set by privsim/mcp-test-runner source repo). The Tier-2 wrapper exposes ≥6 tools mirroring privsim's canonical surface: `run_cargo_tests` (shells out to `cargo nextest run --message-format=json`), `run_pytest` (shells out to `pytest --json-report`), `run_vitest` (shells out to `npx vitest run --reporter=json`), `run_go_tests` (shells out to `go test -json ./...`), `run_bats` (shells out to `bats --formatter json`), `run_jest` (shells out to `npx jest --json`). Wrapper MUST use the official `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (>=1.0.0) NOT a hand-rolled JSON-RPC implementation. Document the Tier-1-vs-Tier-2 decision tree in the manifest's leading comment block AND the RFR's §Tier decision section. Tier-2 selection MUST be triggered ONLY by a documented Tier-1 failure (live capture exit-code != 0 OR `tools/list` returning <6 tools OR malformed JSON-RPC OR ENOENT on the npm package); Tier-1 SHOULD succeed first-attempt per the DEC-0024 §Rationale (privsim/mcp-test-runner is the documented upstream of record on npm).

3. Create plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml mirroring the structure of plugins/testing/mcp-pytest-runner/plugin.toml (P3-W11-F08 / WO-0077): leading multi-line comment with `pinned <DATE>:` block (source URL = https://github.com/privsim/mcp-test-runner for Tier-1 OR the vendored Tier-2 path; npm URL = https://www.npmjs.com/package/test-runner-mcp for Tier-1; pinned ref, install command, advertised tools list with count, transport=stdio, operator-state requirements section [for Tier-1: NONE — npx-managed package self-resolves Node + transitive deps; for Tier-2: NONE — wrapper bundles all test-runner CLIs as Node deps OR documents shell-out to system test-runners]); `[plugin]` section (name='test-runner', version=<pinned semver tag or short-SHA>, description citing master-plan §4.8 line 405 + §5.3 G8 source designation + 'multi-language unified test execution via MCP' per master-plan §4.8); `[capabilities]` section with provides=['testing.cargo.run', 'testing.pytest.run', 'testing.vitest.run', 'testing.go.run', 'testing.bats.run', 'testing.jest.run'] (or analogous testing.* names — namespace established by mcp-pytest-runner in WO-0077, these new capabilities EXTEND it cleanly per master-plan §5.3); languages=['rust', 'python', 'typescript', 'javascript', 'go', 'shell', 'dart'] (test-runner-mcp is multi-language-keyed, contrast with mcp-pytest-runner=['python']); `[transport]` section with type='stdio', command='npx', args=['-y', 'test-runner-mcp@<PINNED-REF>'] for Tier-1 OR command='node' / args=['plugin/test-runner-mcp/dist/index.js'] / cwd=<workspace-root> for Tier-2 (executor selects path at WO execution time per Tier-1-vs-Tier-2 decision); `[lifecycle]` section mirroring F08 exactly (hot_cold=true, idle_timeout_minutes=10).

4. The `transport.command` field MUST be the bare binary name `"npx"` (Tier-1) OR `"node"` (Tier-2) — NOT an absolute path — per WO-0069 lessons §executor: the M1 transport.command-poison mutation pattern relies on swapping to `/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__`, AND absolute paths break across CI/dev environments.

5. Capture the live `tools/list` AT THE SPECIFIC PINNED REF before sealing the manifest's `[capabilities] provides` list and the test's expected-tool-name literal — per WO-0076 §planner lesson 2 + WO-0077 §planner lesson `serverInfo.name asymmetry`. Steps: (a) install via the chosen pinned ref (`npx -y test-runner-mcp@<REF> --help` for Tier-1 warm-up — though `--help` may not be honored by the MCP server; that's fine, the npx fetch primes the cache as a side-effect anyway, mirroring WO-0075 lesson §executor for filesystem-mcp), (b) drive the MCP `initialize` → `notifications/initialized` → `tools/list` handshake via a one-shot Python driver script (mirror the WO-0067/0069/0075/0076/0077/0079/0080 `/tmp/wo-NNNN-capture.py` shape), (c) capture the full advertised tool-list verbatim AND `serverInfo.name` (npm package name `test-runner-mcp` MAY differ from runtime `serverInfo.name` per WO-0077 lesson — document both verbatim in manifest top-of-file rustdoc), (d) embed the captured tool-list in the manifest's `[capabilities] provides` AND the test's SA-tagged panic literal. NB: Per DEC-0024 §Decision point 3, the canonical 6-tool surface (bats/pytest/flutter/jest/go/cargo) MUST be present — if live capture returns <6 tools or a deprecation_notice surface, executor MUST pivot down to the latest preceding ref exposing the canonical surface AND document the pivot as a Disclosed Deviation in the manifest top-of-file rustdoc + RFR.

6. Operator-state-CLI-dependency classification per WO-0076/0077 §planner lesson `distinguish operator-state-CLI-dependent vs self-resolving MCP servers`: test-runner-mcp is **SELF-RESOLVING** at the MCP-server level (npx-managed package handles Node + @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + the wrapper's own deps). However, the wrapper INTERNALLY shells out to `cargo`, `pytest`, `vitest`, `go`, `bats`, `flutter`, `jest` etc. — those constitute SECONDARY operator-state CLI dependencies that are tool-call-time (NOT initialize-time) requirements. Per DEC-0024 §Rationale + §Consequences: the integration test in this WO exercises ONLY the `tools/list` handshake (initialize-time), which does NOT require any of the secondary CLIs to be installed. The verify script's tool-level smoke (per scope_in #11) MAY exercise ONE end-to-end tool-call (e.g., `tools/call run_cargo_tests {...}`) against the workspace's existing rust crates — but ONLY if `cargo --version` succeeds (operator-state short-circuit pattern from WO-0076 Semgrep). Single env-gate: `if [[ "${UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E:-0}" != "0" ]]; then echo '[SKIP] P3-W11-F07: tool-level smoke skipped via UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E'; exit 0; fi` — the cargo-test `tools/list` path (Step 4 of the verify script) remains the load-bearing assertion regardless. NB: WO-0076 §executor lesson `unsafe std::env::set_var('SEMGREP_PATH', ...)` does NOT apply here — test-runner-mcp needs no parent-process env-var mutation for the tools/list smoke; if the verify script's tool-level smoke exercises a secondary CLI, prefer `Command::env` propagation OR a `[[ -x "$(which cargo)" ]]` short-circuit over parent-process env-var mutation.

7. Extend crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs by ADDING a second test `test_runner_manifest_health_check` INSIDE the existing `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block (between the closing brace of the existing `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` at line ~199 and the module's closing `}` at line 200). Mirror the existing test's shape exactly: `#[tokio::test] async fn`, `if skip_via_env() { return; }`, load manifest via `PluginManifest::from_path`, manifest sanity asserts (name='test-runner', transport.kind='stdio', !provides.is_empty(), provides.iter().all(|c| c.starts_with("testing."))), `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout(&manifest, FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS)`, assert health.name='test-runner' (or `serverInfo.name` if it differs — document both in the manifest rustdoc per WO-0077 PyPI-vs-serverInfo.name asymmetry lesson), health.status=Ok, !health.tools.is_empty(), and a load-bearing assertion that one specific advertised tool name is present (executor selects the canonical multi-language tool from the live capture — likely `run_cargo_tests` / `cargo` / `run_pytest` per the upstream's tool-list — and pins on it; rationale comment cites that pinning on the count would force updates on every benign upstream tool addition while pinning on a specific name surfaces upstream tool-surface drift loudly, mirroring the ESLint `lint-files` / Semgrep `semgrep_scan` / mcp-pytest-runner `discover_tests` rationale). Per DEC-0024 §Decision point 3, the test MUST additionally assert `health.tools.len() >= 6` (the canonical multi-language surface — bats/pytest/flutter/jest/go/cargo) as a load-bearing sanity check.

8. ADDITIVELY only — do NOT modify the existing `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` test inside `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` (line ~133 onward in tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs). Confirm via `git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs | grep -E '^-' | grep -vE '^---' | wc -l` returning 0 (zero deletions; only added lines). Per WO-0069 lessons §executor: the existing module's single-test scope must not be mutated by additions. The leading rustdoc comment block at lines 1-85 may be amended ONLY to update the file-level overview (e.g., changing `mcp-pytest-runner G8` → `mcp-pytest-runner + test-runner-mcp G8`, listing both test names in §Tests-wrapped paragraph; removing the historical 'test-runner-mcp (P3-W11-F07) deferred per DEC-0021' note at lines 80-85 and replacing it with a 'test-runner-mcp (P3-W11-F07) revived per DEC-0024' note); preserve all WO-0076/0075 carry-forward citations verbatim.

9. The new test lives INSIDE the existing `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at module root (DEC-0007 frozen-selector module-root placement, carried per WO-0068 lessons §planner 'frozen-test selector substring-match REQUIRES module-root placement'). Confirm `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::test_runner_manifest_health_check` resolves uniquely (substring-matches without requiring `--exact`).

10. Create scripts/devtools/install-test-runner-mcp.sh mirroring scripts/devtools/install-mcp-pytest-runner.sh + scripts/devtools/install-eslint-mcp.sh verbatim in shape: `set -euo pipefail`, `PINNED_TEST_RUNNER_MCP_REF=` constant (SHA or semver per scope_in #1), OK-when-npx-on-PATH branch, MISSING branch with the exact pinned install command (`npx -y test-runner-mcp@<REF> --help` for the Tier-1 warm-up; `--help` may not be honored by the MCP server — that's fine, the npx fetch primes the cache as a side-effect anyway; for Tier-2, the script SHOULD instead `cd plugin/test-runner-mcp && npm install && npm run build` to install the vendored wrapper's deps and compile, then verify import via `node -e "require('./dist/index.js')"`). Always `exit 0` (informational scaffolding — same contract as WO-0069/0072/0075/0076/0077/0079/0080). Tier choice in the install script SHOULD match the manifest's chosen Tier (executor authors both at WO execution time after Tier-1 capture succeeds-or-fails).

11. Replace scripts/verify/P3-W11-F07.sh (currently TODO-stub returning exit 1) by copying scripts/verify/P3-W11-F08.sh OR scripts/verify/P3-W11-F03.sh verbatim and adapting: change pinned-ref constant to the test-runner-mcp commit-or-semver, change the cargo-test selector to `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::test_runner_manifest_health_check`, and adapt the tool-level smoke section per scope_in #6 (single-gate pattern: only UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E check; OPTIONAL secondary `cargo --version` short-circuit if the smoke exercises run_cargo_tests). The smoke MAY exercise at least ONE end-to-end tool-call (e.g., `tools/call run_cargo_tests {...}` against the workspace's existing rust crates OR `tools/call run_pytest {...}` against the fixture python-project) returning at least one structured finding with `test_name/result/duration_ms` keys per F07 spec line: 'unified test execution covers Rust (cargo nextest), Python (pytest), and TypeScript (vitest) via MCP protocol.'. Print `[OK] P3-W11-F07` on green; `[FAIL] P3-W11-F07: <reason>` on red. Always `exit 0` on green / `exit 1` on red. The script remains read-only against tests/fixtures/** (forbidden_paths invariant) — fixture mutations (if any are needed for tool-level smoke per WO-0077 conftest.py fabrication precedent) land in `mktemp -d` tmpdir copy ONLY.

12. Pre-baked mutation contract M1 (test-runner transport.command poison): swap `command = "npx"` → `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"` in plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml; cargo test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::test_runner_manifest_health_check` must FAIL with `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }` (or the analogous spawn-error chain on the platform); restore via `git checkout -- plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml`; confirm md5sum matches the pre-mutation snapshot.

13. Pre-baked mutation contract M2 (expected-tool-name regression): in tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs's new `test_runner_manifest_health_check` test, change the `t == "<canonical_test_runner_tool>"` assertion to a non-existent tool name (e.g., `t == "NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`); the test MUST FAIL with the structured panic carrying the full advertised-tool list (the `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` panic body itself doubles as a diagnostic dump of the live tool surface at the pinned ref). Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs`; confirm md5sum match.

14. Pre-flight word-ban grep MUST cover ONLY production-side files (manifest, install script, verify script, AND if Tier-2 is selected: `plugin/test-runner-mcp/**` vendored wrapper) and EXEMPT integration-test files under `#[cfg(test)]`/`tests/`. The grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-test-runner-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W11-F07.sh [plugin/test-runner-mcp/**]` must return empty before first push. The integration-test file's rustdoc may mention 'Mocking ... is forbidden' inside a forbidden-pattern declaration (per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 carve-out).

15. Pinned-immutable-ref policy: confirm `! grep -qE '"(main|latest|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml` returns empty (the floating-ref class). Frozen semver release tags (e.g., `@0.1.5`) OR git-SHA-pinned URLs (e.g., `git+https://github.com/privsim/mcp-test-runner.git#abc123def456`) are acceptable; bare branch refs OR omitted version specifiers are forbidden. Per master-plan §13 + WO-0069 lesson §executor: floating refs make verifier reruns non-deterministic. DEC-0024 §Decision point 4 EXPLICITLY prefers SHA-pinning (defensive against silent semver violations on a 16-star upstream).

16. The integration-test file's `mod g8_plugin_manifests` block must use `tokio::process::Command` (not `std::process::Command`) for any helper subprocess invocations introduced by this WO — per WO-0075 §executor lesson W1 + WO-0076 pre-emptive carve-out. The existing `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` uses `tokio::process::Command` already (via `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` internally); the new test_runner test SHOULD follow the same minimalist shape. NB: F07's spec calls for 'unified test execution covers Rust (cargo nextest), Python (pytest), and TypeScript (vitest) via MCP protocol' — the verify script's tool-level smoke (NOT the cargo test) is the right place for the run_cargo_tests / run_pytest / run_vitest end-to-end assertions. The cargo test's load-bearing assertion is the daemon-side spawn + tools/list handshake. Pre-flight `! grep -qE 'std::process::Command' crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs` (covers both tests) before first push.

17. `unsafe std::env::set_var` IS FORBIDDEN in this WO's test code. test-runner-mcp does NOT need parent-process env-var mutation for the tools/list smoke (contrast with WO-0076 Semgrep which used `unsafe set_var("SEMGREP_PATH", ...)` — that was the W1+W2 debt; this WO sidesteps it by virtue of test-runner-mcp being self-resolving at the MCP-server level). NEW unsafe blocks must NOT be introduced by this WO. Per WO-0076 §verifier lesson 'unsafe block without ADR cite — soft critic-warning category'.

18. DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out applies to the augmentation of tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs (single small commit adding ONE test inside the existing `mod` block — likely <100 LOC). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0080 commit precedent (ruff g7 augmentation) AND WO-0079 commit `c448dfc` precedent (graphiti g3 augmentation) in the relevant commit body. Do NOT split the new test into multiple commits.

19. §15.2 tracing spans do NOT apply to plugin-manifest landings — these features add data files (TOML manifests) + integration tests + helper scripts, NOT new orchestration code. Do NOT introduce new `#[tracing::instrument(...)]` annotations under this WO. Cite WO-0067 lessons §planner #2 ('pure-deterministic / data-only modules: §15.2 tracing does NOT apply') as precedent.

20. WO-0069 lessons §planner #2 carve-out (pre-emptive API-key-gate for plugins requiring keys) does NOT apply here — test-runner-mcp has NO API-key requirement (self-resolving multi-language test-runner, no LLM, no external service beyond the npx-managed package). The verify script's gate is single-axis: `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` only.

21. WO-0069 lessons §planner #3 carve-out (eventual-consistency OR-disjunction): test-runner-mcp's tool-level smoke is fully synchronous — `cargo nextest run` / `pytest --json-report` / `vitest run` return deterministic structured findings on first invocation. AND-conjunctive assertions are fine. No OR-disjunction needed (contrast with WO-0079 graphiti's eventual-consistency vector index).

22. AC25 reworded per WO-0070 lessons §planner #4: 'feat branch carries zero merge commits' (using `git log feat/WO-0081-test-runner-mcp-plugin-manifest ^main --merges` returning empty), NOT the 'feat..main = 0' form. Same spirit, more precise wording — tolerates 1-commit divergence between feat and main caused by gate-side critic-report commits between executor RFR push and verifier startup.

23. Carry-forward standing protocol (36 WOs deep, WO-0058..WO-0080): coverage-gate.sh sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround — `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the authoritative measurement; the gate-script `coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 85 75` reporting `line=0%` is the known sccache interaction. Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-{core,daemon,embeddings}` lines in phase-3.sh output per scope_out + AC23 standing protocol.

24. Carry-forward standing protocol: AC30/AC31 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake (3 open escalations: 20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md, 20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md, 20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md). Executor runs constituent cargo-test + clippy + MCP-smoke + Serena-docker + diagnostics-bridge sub-checks in isolation; verifier re-runs the gate-script entry point from a clean session. Pre-existing standing scope_out item; awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO.

25. Verifier MUST NOT set `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` OR `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` envs (per WO-0076 scope_in #12 + WO-0079 scope_in #25 + WO-0080 scope_in #25 precedent extended to this WO).

26. RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include the M1/M2 mutation contract section with: (a) named file path, (b) the exact patch (e.g., `s/command = "npx"/command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"/`), (c) the targeted cargo-test selector, (d) the expected panic line, (e) the restore command (`git checkout -- <path>`), (f) the pre-mutation md5 snapshot path (`/tmp/wo-0081-<file>-orig.md5`). Mirror the WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0077/0079/0080 RFR Mutation contract section verbatim in shape.

27. Pre-flight `which npx` AND `which node` before first push — the integration tests will spawn from PATH, and missing `npx` produces false-positive `PluginError::Spawn { kind: NotFound }` which masks real M1 detection. Per WO-0069 lessons §verifier #2 + WO-0075/0076/0077/0079/0080 carry. NPX is the load-bearing PATH binary for Tier-1; node is load-bearing for Tier-2.

28. Pre-baked md5 snapshots: take `md5sum plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0081-test-runner-orig.md5` AND `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0081-g8tests-orig.md5` BEFORE applying any mutation. Confirm restoration via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0081-<name>-orig.md5` returning OK after each `git checkout --` restore.

29. WO-0072 (codegraphcontext G4 solo-feature WO) + WO-0077 (mcp-pytest-runner G8 solo-feature WO) + WO-0079 (graphiti G3 solo-feature deferral-revival WO) + WO-0080 (ruff G7 solo-feature deferral-revival WO) are the canonical references for the SOLO-FEATURE plugin-manifest WO shape. Same shape applied here: ONE feature, ONE manifest, ONE install script, ONE replacement verify script, ONE additive test in an existing peer test file (g8_plugin_manifests.rs), ONE M1 + ONE M2 mutation contract. Avoid 2-feature WOs that would mix groups (G8 + something else).

30. F07 has `attempts=0` (DEC-0021 deferral was admin-only per the post-DEC-0022/0023/0024 lessons — no executor cycles consumed). This WO is the **first attempt**; clean execution expected per the SEXTUPLE-verified G3→G4→G5→G6→G7→G8 paired-manifest template. The blocking factor in DEC-0021 was 'no canonical PyPI/GitHub upstream' — that's now resolved by the `test-runner-mcp` npm install path discovered via broader npm-registry search per DEC-0024 §Context (the prior planner's triad searched PyPI + GitHub-repo-by-exact-name but NOT npm). NO architectural ambiguities remain.

31. Apply WO-0076/0077/0080 §executor lessons #1-#5 verbatim — they all carried forward. #1 (no kebab→snake translation; live capture documented in manifest comments — executor pins on the upstream's verbatim emission), #2 (no `--mcp` for warm-up; install script uses `npx ... --help`), #3 (`UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` documented in test rustdoc + verify script — extends existing G8 env-var, NO new env-var needed), #4 (python deadline polling in verify script: 90s init / 30s tools-list / 60s tools-call — test-runner-mcp tools/list is fast, ~1-2s on warm cache; cold-cache npx fetch may take up to ~30s on first run hence FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS=120_000 already present), #5 (fixture copy BEFORE invoking — verify script uses file-by-file copy with `__pycache__` / `.pytest_cache` / `target` / `node_modules` prune per WO-0077 §executor lesson if any pycache/build dirs exist in the workspace).

32. Npm package name vs serverInfo.name asymmetry capture per WO-0077 §planner lesson `Capture BOTH PyPI/npm package name AND serverInfo.name`: npm package = `test-runner-mcp`; runtime `serverInfo.name` MAY differ (could be `test-runner-mcp` OR `mcp-test-runner` OR `multi-language-test-runner` — executor's live capture is the source of truth). Document BOTH names verbatim in manifest top-of-file rustdoc when they differ. Manifest's `[plugin] name` field is `test-runner` (the conceptual capability per UCIL's plugin namespace, mirroring the `mcp-pytest-runner` → `[plugin] name = mcp-pytest-runner` convention but with the cleaner short-name `test-runner` since the upstream package already carries the `-mcp` suffix); the install string references the npm package; the test asserts on the runtime serverInfo.name.

33. Cold-cache npx fetch budget per WO-0077 §executor lesson: `FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000` already set in tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs at line ~100 — covers the cold-cache npx fetch (resolves test-runner-mcp + @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + transitive deps + downloads npm tarballs) which takes up to ~30s; subsequent runs are <1s. NO change to FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS needed.

34. Test-runner-mcp is SELF-RESOLVING at the MCP-server level per WO-0076/0077 §planner lesson `distinguish operator-state-CLI-dependent vs self-resolving`. NO `resolve_working_test_runner()` helper, NO operator-state short-circuit on missing `test-runner-mcp` binary, NO `TEST_RUNNER_PATH` env var. The Tier-1 install path (test-runner-mcp on npm) bundles all deps via npx-managed package. The Tier-2 vendored wrapper (if executor falls back) bundles deps in its own package.json identically. The W1+W2 unsafe-set_var debt from WO-0076 dissolves naturally for this WO. NB: the SECONDARY operator-state CLI deps (cargo, pytest, vitest, go, bats, flutter, jest) are tool-call-time requirements, NOT initialize-time — the initialize-time `tools/list` handshake exercises NONE of them.

35. F07 spec acceptance per master-plan §4.8 + feature description: 'unified test execution covers Rust (cargo nextest), Python (pytest), and TypeScript (vitest) via MCP protocol.' The verify script's tool-level smoke MAY exercise this end-to-end: invoke `tools/call run_cargo_tests` (or the upstream's actual tool name from live capture) against the workspace's existing crates, OR `tools/call run_pytest` against the fixture python-project, OR `tools/call run_vitest` against an adapter typescript package. Assert at least one structured finding with `test_name/result/duration_ms` keys is returned. This is OPTIONAL per scope_in #11 — the cargo-test `tools/list` handshake (initialize-time) is the LOAD-BEARING assertion for F07 manifest landing; tool-call-time smokes are nice-to-have but require the secondary CLI to be present (`cargo --version` / `pytest --version` / `vitest --version` short-circuit). Fixture-pristineness invariant holds; mutations (if any) land in `mktemp -d` tmpdir copy ONLY per WO-0076 ESLint `bad.js` precedent + WO-0077 conftest.py precedent.

36. The DEC-0024 chain DEC-0019 → DEC-0020 → DEC-0021 reactive deferral, then DEC-0022 → DEC-0023 → DEC-0024 revival pattern is now twice-applied AND fully resolved. With WO-0079 (graphiti revival per DEC-0022) + WO-0080 (ruff revival per DEC-0023) + WO-0081 (test-runner-mcp revival per DEC-0024) all landed, the post-DEC-0022/0023/0024 supersedure noted in the phase-log line 636-641 is fully discharged. Phase 3 reaches 21/45 = 46.7% complete after F07 lands. Subsequent planner passes (WO-0082+) MUST resume the regular Phase 3 feature-list traversal targeting the remaining 24 unfinished features (P3-W9-F11 incremental test suite, P3-W10-F01 Aider repo-map, P3-W10-F04 G5 Context query, P3-W10-F09..F18 dependency-cruiser/Zoekt + bonus-context selector + multi-tier merging + warm processors + architecture/dependencies/blast_radius MCP tools, P3-W11-F01/F05/F06 G7 quality pipeline + severity-merge + quality_issues table, P3-W11-F09..F16 G8 test discovery + check_quality + review_changes + feedback loop + integration test suites + type_check + full-pipeline integration suite). The deferral chain is administratively closed.

3. Do NOT modify the existing `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` test inside `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` (line ~133 onward) — only ADD the second test_runner test. 5. Do NOT wire the new manifest into any `PluginManager::initialize_all` / startup-discovery path beyond what already exists — the WO-0044 + WO-0069 + WO-0076 + WO-0077 manifests are loaded on-demand by the integration tests via `PluginManifest::from_path(&manifest_path)`, and the new manifest should be loadable the same way without daemon-startup changes.
6. Do NOT introduce or modify any LLM-provider or embedding-provider configuration (test-runner-mcp has no LLM dependency).
7. Do NOT introduce graph-DB or external-service sidecar provisioning — test-runner-mcp is pure-Node multi-language test-runner shim, NO services beyond the npx-managed package.
8. Do NOT introduce any cross-WO dependency wiring (downstream WOs consume F07; that's their scope — F09/F10 G8 production-wiring deferrals).
9. Do NOT add any new `tracing::instrument` annotations to source code (per scope_in #19).
10. Do NOT add or modify entries in `ucil-build/feature-list.json` — verifier flips `passes` via `scripts/flip-feature.sh`.

11. Do NOT modify any file under `tests/fixtures/**` — verify scripts are read-only against the fixture (per WO-0069 forbidden_paths). Any tmpdir-fabrication for tool-level smoke (e.g., conftest.py for run_pytest smoke per WO-0077 precedent) lands in the `mktemp -d` tmpdir copy ONLY (per scope_in #35).

13. Do NOT prematurely select Tier-2 (vendored TS wrapper) without first attempting Tier-1 (npx test-runner-mcp). DEC-0024 §Decision point 4 grants Tier-2 fallback authority IN THE SAME WO only if Tier-1 fails — Tier-1 is the default per DEC-0024 §Rationale (privsim/mcp-test-runner is the documented upstream of record on npm; package URL https://www.npmjs.com/package/test-runner-mcp is reachable at planner time).
14. Do NOT introduce a new `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_*` env-var — extend the existing `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` from WO-0077 verbatim (single env-var per group; new tests reuse the existing gate).

15. Do NOT introduce `unsafe std::env::set_var` blocks (WO-0076 W1+W2 debt). test-runner-mcp is self-resolving at the MCP-server level — no parent-process env-var mutation needed for the tools/list smoke (per scope_in #17 + #34).
16. Do NOT modify scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh OR scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh — the standing carry-forwards (36 WOs deep) remain out-of-scope.

17. Do NOT split the `plugin/test-runner-mcp/` Tier-2 vendored wrapper across multiple commits if Tier-2 is selected — the ~200-LOC TS wrapper + package.json + tsconfig.json is a single coherent unit per DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out.

18. Do NOT introduce ESLint-style `eslint.config.js` or pytest-style `conftest.py` config fabrication outside the tmpdir copy — the tools/list handshake (the load-bearing assertion) requires NO per-project config. If executor exercises a tool-level smoke for run_pytest in the verify script and finds the upstream MCP server requires a conftest.py or pyproject.toml, fabricate it identically to WO-0076/0077 patterns (tmpdir copy ONLY).

2. scripts/devtools/install-test-runner-mcp.sh exists, is executable (chmod +x), shells out to `npx -y test-runner-mcp@<REF> --help` (Tier-1) OR `cd plugin/test-runner-mcp && npm install && npm run build` + import probe (Tier-2), and exits 0 unconditionally (informational-only contract).

5. cargo test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::test_runner_manifest_health_check` resolves uniquely (substring-match without --exact), green from a `cargo clean` baseline.
